title: This 1 Little-Known Cannabis Stock Is Up Nearly 90% in 2025. Is It Too Late to Buy Shares?
text: Curaleaf stock has surged nearly 90% in 2025 following President Trump's comments suggesting marijuana reclassification could happen within weeks. The company has benefited from positive sentiment around potential federal cannabis policy changes. In the June quarter, Curaleaf reported revenue of $315 million, down 8% year-over-year, as domestic price pressures continue to squeeze margins, while the international division grew by 17% sequentially and 62% year-over-year. The stark contrast between domestic challenges and international growth is a key story for the company. Investors have recognized Curaleaf's international diversification strategy as a potential hedge against domestic market pressures. The company's ability to grow internationally at much higher rates than its domestic operations suggests a multi-year growth runway as international markets mature. Trading at recent levels near $3.89, the stock offers exposure to both cannabis market recovery and international expansion themes.
